description: Add a push-based notifyReadinessChanged() API to the plugin SDK that allows adapters to signal the Chrome extension when their readiness state has changed. Currently, readiness is only detected by a 30-second poll or tab navigation events. When a user logs in/out via an SPA flow (no URL change, no page reload), the state transition takes up to 30 seconds to detect. This feature adds a postMessage channel from the MAIN world adapter to the ISOLATED world relay to the background service worker, triggering an immediate re-probe of isReady() for the affected plugin+tab.

userStories.title: Add notifyReadinessChanged() to the plugin SDK
userStories.description: Add a notifyReadinessChanged() function to the plugin SDK that posts a window.postMessage with type 'opentabs:readiness-changed' and the plugin name. This is the API plugins call when they detect auth state changes.
acceptanceCriteria: notifyReadinessChanged posts a window.postMessage with type 'opentabs:readiness-changed', the plugin name, and the nonce from globalThis.__openTabs._readinessNonce
notifyReadinessChanged is a no-op (no throw) when called outside the browser (globalThis.__openTabs is undefined)

userStories.title: Wire notifyReadinessChanged in the IIFE wrapper and ISOLATED world relay
userStories.description: Update the IIFE wrapper (build.ts) to set up a _notifyReadinessChanged closure on globalThis.__openTabs, generate a separate _readinessNonce, and update the ISOLATED world relay (iife-injection.ts) to listen for 'opentabs:readiness-changed' messages and forward them to the background via chrome.runtime.sendMessage.
acceptanceCriteria: The IIFE wrapper sets globalThis.__openTabs._notifyReadinessChanged as a function that posts window.postMessage with type 'opentabs:readiness-changed', the plugin name, and the readiness nonce

The ISOLATED world relay (injectLogRelay or a new injectReadinessRelay) generates a separate crypto.randomUUID() nonce for readiness, validates incoming messages against it, and forwards valid messages to the background as chrome.runtime.sendMessage({ type: 'plugin:readinessChanged', plugin, tabId: sender.tab.id })
The readiness nonce is injected into MAIN world on globalThis.__openTabs._readinessNonce, separate from the log nonce
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.ts (IIFE wrapper â€” add _notifyReadinessChanged closure)
- platform/browser-extension/src/iife-injection.ts (add readiness relay alongside log relay)

Context:
The log relay pattern is the template. In `iife-injection.ts`, `injectLogRelay` (line 78-141) does:
1. Generate nonce via `crypto.randomUUID()`
2. Inject ISOLATED world listener that validates nonce and forwards `opentabs:plugin-logs` to background
3. Inject MAIN world script that sets `globalThis.__openTabs._logNonce = nonce`

The readiness relay follows the same pattern but for `opentabs:readiness-changed` messages.

Changes to `iife-injection.ts`:
Add a new function `injectReadinessRelay` (or extend `injectLogRelay` to handle both message types â€” whichever is cleaner). It should:
1. Generate a separate `readinessNonce` via `crypto.randomUUID()`
2. Inject an ISOLATED world listener for `opentabs:readiness-changed` that validates the nonce and sends `chrome.runtime.sendMessage({ type: 'plugin:readinessChanged', plugin: data.plugin })`
3. Inject the nonce into MAIN world: `globalThis.__openTabs._readinessNonce = readinessNonce`

Note: the `tabId` is NOT available in the content script context. The background handler will get `sender.tab?.id` from the `chrome.runtime.onMessage` sender. So the relay just forwards `{ type: 'plugin:readinessChanged', plugin }` â€” the background adds the tabId.

Call `injectReadinessRelay` from `injectAdapterFile` (line 196, right after `injectLogRelay`).

Changes to `build.ts`:
In the IIFE wrapper code (around line 730, after the log transport setup), add:
```js
const readinessNonce = ot._readinessNonce;
if (readinessNonce) {
  ot._notifyReadinessChanged = () => {
    window.postMessage({ type: 'opentabs:readiness-changed', plugin: ${name}, nonce: readinessNonce }, '*');
  };
}
```

This goes AFTER the `globalThis.__openTabs` setup but BEFORE the adapter hash/freeze step. The `${name}` is the JSON-stringified plugin name already available in the template context.

Constraints:
- The readiness nonce MUST be separate from the log nonce â€” do not reuse `_logNonce`
- The nonce validation in the ISOLATED world MUST match the pattern in `injectLogRelay` â€” compare `data.nonce` against the generated nonce
- Do NOT modify the existing log relay â€” add the readiness relay alongside it
- The relay message to background must use type `'plugin:readinessChanged'` (this is what US-003 handles)

userStories.title: Handle plugin:readinessChanged in the background and trigger immediate re-probe
userStories.description: Add a handler in the background service worker for the 'plugin:readinessChanged' message from content scripts. The handler extracts the plugin name and sender tab ID, then triggers an immediate probeTabReadiness for that specific plugin+tab, updating the tab state and notifying the MCP server if the state changed.
acceptanceCriteria: The handler calls probeTabReadiness for the specific plugin and sender tab ID
If the probe result changes the plugin's aggregate state, sendTabStateNotification is called
The handler is a no-op if the plugin name is not recognized or the tab ID is missing

userStories.title: Add E2E test tool and test for notifyReadinessChanged
userStories.description: Add a new tool to the e2e-test plugin that calls notifyReadinessChanged(), and write E2E tests verifying the full pipeline: SDK call â†’ postMessage â†’ ISOLATED relay â†’ background handler â†’ re-probe â†’ tab state change reported to MCP server.
acceptanceCriteria: e2e-test plugin has a new tool (e.g., 'sdk_notify_readiness_changed' or similar) that calls notifyReadinessChanged() from the SDK
E2E test verifies that calling the tool while isReady() returns true does NOT cause a state change (state was already ready)
E2E test verifies the full postMessage pipeline works by confirming the tool completes without error and the state remains consistent
userStories.notes:
Files:
- plugins/e2e-test/src/tools/sdk-notify-readiness-changed.ts (new tool)
- plugins/e2e-test/src/index.ts (register the new tool)
- e2e/notify-readiness-changed.e2e.ts (new E2E test file)

Context:
The e2e-test plugin is a special plugin used exclusively for E2E testing. It registers tools that exercise SDK features. The existing pattern for SDK test tools (e.g., `sdk-get-local-storage.ts`, `sdk-wait-for-selector.ts`) is:

```ts
import { defineTool, notifyReadinessChanged } from '@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk';

export const sdkNotifyReadinessChanged = defineTool({
  name: 'sdk_notify_readiness_changed',
  description: 'Tests notifyReadinessChanged â€” calls the SDK function and returns success',
  parameters: {},
  handler: async () => {
    notifyReadinessChanged();
    return { ok: true };
  },
});
```

Register in `plugins/e2e-test/src/index.ts` â€” add to the tools array following the existing pattern.

For the E2E test, follow the pattern in `plugin-logging.e2e.ts` (tests the postMessage pipeline). The test should:
1. Set up the tool test with `setupToolTest`
2. Verify the e2e-test plugin is in 'ready' state
3. Call `e2e-test_sdk_notify_readiness_changed` tool
4. Verify the tool succeeds
5. Verify the plugin is still in 'ready' state (state should not change since it was already ready)

A more thorough test would verify state transitions (unavailable â†’ ready), but that requires the e2e-test plugin's `isReady()` to dynamically change its return value, which is complex. The basic pipeline test (verifying the message flows without error) is sufficient for this story.

To test the unavailable â†’ ready transition, consider having the e2e-test plugin's isReady check a flag in localStorage that the test can toggle:
- Set localStorage flag to make isReady return false
- Navigate to the test page â†’ plugin becomes unavailable
- Set localStorage flag to make isReady return true
- Call the notify tool (via browser_execute_script since the plugin is unavailable and tools won't dispatch)
- Wait for the plugin to become ready

Actually, the e2e-test plugin's isReady currently always returns true (it just checks if the adapter is present). To test the transition properly, you'd need to modify isReady to check a localStorage flag. This is optional â€” the basic pipeline test is the minimum.

Constraints:
- The e2e-test plugin's package.json must have `@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk` as a dependency â€” it already does
- The new tool must follow the existing naming convention: `sdk_notify_readiness_changed` (underscored, matching the file name pattern)
- The E2E test file should follow the existing pattern in `plugin-logging.e2e.ts`
